news_summary: Today's technology news highlights breakthrough AI advancements accelerating scientific discovery, enhancing human creativity, and transforming biological research and understanding of marine life.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité technologique d'aujourd'hui met en avant des avancées révolutionnaires en matière d'intelligence artificielle qui accélèrent les découvertes scientifiques, stimulent la créativité humaine et transforment la recherche biologique ainsi que notre compréhension de la vie marine.
news_summary_es: Las noticias tecnológicas de hoy destacan los avances revolucionarios en inteligencia artificial que aceleran los descubrimientos científicos, potencian la creatividad humana y transforman la investigación biológica y la comprensión de la vida marina.

articles.title: THOR AI solves a 100-year-old physics problem in seconds
articles.summary: A new AI framework called THOR is transforming how scientists calculate the behavior of atoms inside materials. Instead of relying on slow simulations that take weeks of supercomputer time, the system uses tensor network mathematics and machine-learning models to solve the problem directly. The approach can compute key thermodynamic properties hundreds of times faster while preserving accuracy. Researchers say this could accelerate discoveries in materials science, physics, and chemistry.
articles.content:
Researchers at The University of New Mexico and Los Alamos National Laboratory have introduced a new computational approach designed to solve one of the most difficult problems in statistical physics. Their system, called the Tensors for High-dimensional Object Representation (THOR) AI framework, uses tensor network algorithms to handle extremely large mathematical calculations known as configurational integrals, along with the partial differential equations needed to analyze materials.

These calculations are essential for predicting the thermodynamic and mechanical behavior of materials. To make the system more powerful, the researchers combined the framework with machine learning potentials that capture how atoms interact and move. This integration allows scientists to model materials accurately and efficiently across a wide range of physical environments.

"The configurational integral -- which captures particle interactions -- is notoriously difficult and time-consuming to evaluate, particularly in materials science applications involving extreme pressures or phase transitions," said Los Alamos senior AI scientist Boian Alexandrov, who led the project. "Accurately determining the thermodynamic behavior deepens our scientific understanding of statistical mechanics and informs key areas such as metallurgy."

Why Configurational Integrals Are So Difficult to Compute

For decades, researchers have depended on indirect computational techniques such as molecular dynamics and Monte Carlo simulations to estimate configurational integrals. These methods attempt to reproduce the movement of atoms by simulating enormous numbers of interactions over extended periods.

The main obstacle comes from what scientists call the "curse of dimensionality." As the number of variables grows, the complexity of the calculations increases exponentially. Even the most advanced supercomputers struggle with this challenge. As a result, simulations often run for weeks while still providing only approximate answers.

Dimiter Petsev, a professor in the UNM Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering, frequently collaborates with Alexandrov on materials science research. When Alexandrov described the computational strategy his team had developed, Petsev realized that the technique might offer a way to directly evaluate the configurational integral in statistical mechanics.

"Traditionally, solving the configurational integral directly has been considered impossible because the integral often involves dimensions on the order of thousands. Classical integration techniques would require computational times exceeding the age of the universe, even with modern computers," Petsev said. "Tensor network methods, however, offer a new standard of accuracy and efficiency against which other approaches can be benchmarked."

THOR AI Makes High-Dimensional Calculations Practical

THOR AI converts this seemingly unmanageable problem into something that can be solved efficiently. It does this by expressing the massive high-dimensional dataset of the integrand as a sequence of smaller connected pieces. The framework relies on a mathematical strategy known as "tensor train cross interpolation" to achieve this compression.

Researchers also developed a specialized version of the method that detects key crystal symmetries within the material. By identifying these patterns, THOR AI dramatically reduces the amount of computation required. Calculations that once demanded thousands of hours can now be completed in seconds without sacrificing accuracy.

Faster Simulations for Materials Science and Physics

The team tested THOR AI on several materials systems. These included metals such as copper, noble gases under extreme pressure such as argon in crystalline state, and the complex solid-solid phase transition of tin. In each case, the new method reproduced results previously obtained from advanced Los Alamos simulations while running more than 400 times faster.

The framework also integrates smoothly with modern machine learning atomic models, allowing it to analyze materials under a wide variety of conditions. Because of this flexibility, researchers say THOR AI could become a valuable tool across materials science, physics and chemistry.

"This breakthrough replaces century-old simulations and approximations of configurational integral with a first-principles calculation," said Duc Truong, Los Alamos scientist and lead author of the study published in Physical Review Materials. "THOR AI opens the door to faster discoveries and a deeper understanding of materials."

The THOR Project is available on GitHub.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant breakthrough in computational physics and materials science, where the THOR AI framework solves a century-old complex problem efficiently and accurately. This advancement has broad implications for accelerating scientific discoveries in materials science, physics, and chemistry, benefiting many fields and society at large. The article provides detailed context on the problem, the innovation, and its impact, fulfilling the criteria for inspiring good news.
articles.personality_title: New AI solves century-old physics problem in seconds
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists have long struggled to understand how atoms behave inside materials because the math involved is extremely complex. Traditional methods require supercomputers running for weeks to get approximate answers.

**What happened** – Researchers at the University of New Mexico and Los Alamos National Laboratory created an AI system called THOR. This system uses advanced math called tensor networks combined with machine learning to solve difficult physics equations directly. THOR can calculate important properties of materials hundreds of times faster than before without losing accuracy.

**Impact** – This is important because it makes a problem that used to take weeks of supercomputer time now solvable in seconds. It helps scientists better predict how materials like metals and crystals behave under different conditions. This faster and more precise method could speed up discoveries in physics, chemistry, and materials science.

**What's next step** – The researchers have made THOR available on GitHub so other scientists can use and improve it. They plan to apply it to more materials and conditions, potentially uncovering new insights about how matter works.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The THOR AI framework dramatically speeds up solving complex physics problems, opening new paths for research in materials and science.
articles.personality_title_fr: Une nouvelle IA résout un problème de physique vieux d’un siècle en quelques secondes
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Comprendre le comportement des atomes dans les matériaux est difficile à cause des calculs très complexes. Les méthodes traditionnelles demandent des semaines de calcul sur des superordinateurs pour obtenir des réponses approximatives.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’Université du Nouveau-Mexique et du Laboratoire national de Los Alamos ont créé un système d’IA appelé THOR. Ce système utilise des mathématiques avancées nommées réseaux de tenseurs combinés à l’apprentissage automatique pour résoudre directement des équations physiques difficiles. THOR peut calculer des propriétés importantes des matériaux des centaines de fois plus vite qu’avant, sans perdre en précision.

**Impact** – C’est important car un problème qui prenait des semaines peut maintenant être résolu en quelques secondes. Cela aide les scientifiques à mieux prédire le comportement des matériaux comme les métaux et les cristaux dans différentes conditions. Cette méthode plus rapide et précise peut accélérer les découvertes en physique, chimie et science des matériaux.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs ont rendu THOR disponible sur GitHub pour que d’autres scientifiques puissent l’utiliser et l’améliorer. Ils prévoient de l’appliquer à plus de matériaux et de conditions, ce qui pourrait révéler de nouvelles connaissances sur la matière.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Le système d’IA THOR accélère énormément la résolution de problèmes complexes en physique, ouvrant de nouvelles voies pour la recherche en matériaux et en sciences.
articles.personality_title_es: Nueva IA resuelve problema de física centenario en segundos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Entender cómo se comportan los átomos dentro de los materiales es difícil porque las matemáticas son muy complejas. Los métodos tradicionales requieren supercomputadoras trabajando semanas para obtener respuestas aproximadas.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad de Nuevo México y el Laboratorio Nacional de Los Álamos crearon un sistema de IA llamado THOR. Este sistema usa matemáticas avanzadas llamadas redes tensoriales junto con aprendizaje automático para resolver directamente ecuaciones físicas difíciles. THOR puede calcular propiedades importantes de materiales cientos de veces más rápido que antes sin perder precisión.

**Impacto** – Esto es importante porque un problema que antes tomaba semanas ahora se puede resolver en segundos. Ayuda a los científicos a predecir mejor cómo se comportan materiales como metales y cristales bajo diferentes condiciones. Este método más rápido y preciso podría acelerar descubrimientos en física, química y ciencia de materiales.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores han puesto THOR disponible en GitHub para que otros científicos lo usen y mejoren. Planean aplicarlo a más materiales y condiciones, lo que podría revelar nuevos conocimientos sobre la materia.

**Resumen en una frase** – El sistema de IA THOR acelera enormemente la resolución de problemas complejos en física, abriendo nuevas oportunidades para la investigación en materiales y ciencia.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of an intricate, glowing lattice of interconnected geometric shapes symbolizing tensor networks, seamlessly transforming a vast, complex web of tiny particles and crystalline structures—such as copper and argon atoms—into a clear, radiant, and harmonious pattern that conveys breakthrough clarity and accelerated computation in materials science.

articles.title: Scientists discover AI can make humans more creative
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is often portrayed as a tool that replaces human work, but new research from Swansea University suggests a far more exciting role: creative collaborator. In a large study with more than 800 participants designing virtual cars, researchers found that AI-generated design galleries sparked deeper engagement, longer exploration, and better results.
articles.content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) is commonly viewed as a technology designed to automate work and potentially replace human labor. However, new research from Swansea University offers a different perspective. The findings suggest that AI can also function as a creative collaborator that encourages exploration, engagement, and inspiration.

Researchers from the University's Computer Science Department carried out one of the largest studies so far examining how people work alongside AI during creative design tasks. More than 800 participants joined an online experiment where they used an AI-supported system to design virtual cars.

How AI Generated Diverse Design Ideas

Rather than quietly optimizing designs behind the scenes, the system used a method called MAP-Elites to produce visual galleries filled with many different design possibilities. These galleries showed a wide spectrum of car concepts, including highly effective designs, unusual ideas, and even some intentionally flawed options.

Turing Fellow Dr. Sean Walton, Associate Professor of Computer Science and the study's lead author, explained: "People often think of AI as something that speeds up tasks or improves efficiency, but our findings suggest something far more interesting. When people were shown AI-generated design suggestions, they spent more time on the task, produced better designs and felt more involved. It was not just about efficiency. It was about creativity and collaboration."

Why Traditional AI Evaluation May Be Too Limited

The study, published in the ACM journal Transactions on Interactive Intelligent Systems, also highlights a problem with how AI design tools are typically assessed. Standard metrics often focus on simple behaviors, such as how frequently users click on or copy AI suggestions. According to the researchers, these measures overlook important aspects of the experience, including how the technology influences people's thoughts, emotions, and willingness to explore new ideas.

The Swansea researchers argue that AI systems should be evaluated using broader methods that capture these deeper effects. Understanding how AI shapes human thinking and engagement could provide a more complete picture of its impact.

Why Imperfect Ideas Can Boost Creativity

Dr. Walton emphasized that variety in AI-generated output played a crucial role in the experiment. "Our study highlights the importance of diversity in AI output. Participants responded most positively to galleries that included a wide variety of ideas, including bad ones! These helped them move beyond their initial assumptions and explore a broader design space. This structured diversity prevented early fixation and encouraged creative risk-taking.

"As AI becomes increasingly embedded in creative fields, from engineering and architecture to music and game design, understanding how humans and intelligent systems work together is essential. As the technology evolves, the question is not only what AI can do but how it can help us think, create and collaborate more effectively."
articles.reasoning: The article reports on a significant research breakthrough demonstrating that AI can enhance human creativity by acting as a collaborative partner rather than just an efficiency tool. The study involved a large sample size and provides meaningful insights into how AI-generated diverse ideas can foster deeper engagement and better creative outcomes. This has broad implications for multiple creative fields and advances understanding of human-AI collaboration.
articles.personality_title: AI helps people become more creative by offering diverse ideas
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Many people think artificial intelligence (AI) is mainly used to do tasks faster or replace human jobs. But new research from Swansea University shows AI can also help people be more creative by working with them.

**What happened** – Researchers at Swansea University asked over 800 people to design virtual cars using a special AI system. This system showed users many different design ideas, including some unusual or even imperfect ones. The study found that when people saw these AI-generated galleries, they spent more time designing, felt more involved, and created better car designs.

**Impact** – This study is important because it shows AI can do more than just speed up work. By giving a variety of ideas, including some “bad” ones, AI helps people think in new ways and take creative risks. It also points out that usual ways of measuring AI’s help, like counting clicks, miss how AI affects thinking and creativity.

**What's next step** – Researchers suggest that future AI tools should focus on providing diverse ideas to encourage creativity. They also want to develop new ways to measure how AI influences how people think and create. This could change how AI is used in many creative jobs like designing, music, or games.

**One-sentence takeaway** – AI can boost human creativity by offering a wide range of ideas that inspire deeper thinking and better designs.
articles.personality_title_fr: L’IA aide les gens à être plus créatifs grâce à des idées variées
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Beaucoup pensent que l’intelligence artificielle (IA) sert surtout à faire les tâches plus vite ou à remplacer les humains. Mais une nouvelle recherche de l’Université de Swansea montre que l’IA peut aussi aider les gens à être plus créatifs en collaborant avec eux.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de Swansea ont demandé à plus de 800 personnes de concevoir des voitures virtuelles avec un système d’IA spécial. Ce système montrait aux utilisateurs plusieurs idées de design, y compris des idées inhabituelles ou imparfaites. L’étude a montré que les personnes qui voyaient ces galeries créées par l’IA passaient plus de temps à créer, se sentaient plus impliquées et faisaient de meilleurs designs.

**Impact** – Cette étude est importante car elle montre que l’IA peut faire plus que simplement accélérer le travail. En proposant une variété d’idées, même « mauvaises », l’IA aide les gens à penser autrement et à prendre des risques créatifs. Elle souligne aussi que les façons habituelles de mesurer l’aide de l’IA, comme compter les clics, ne montrent pas comment l’IA influence la pensée et la créativité.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs suggèrent que les futurs outils d’IA devraient offrir des idées variées pour encourager la créativité. Ils veulent aussi développer de nouvelles méthodes pour mesurer comment l’IA influence la pensée et la création. Cela pourrait changer l’utilisation de l’IA dans de nombreux métiers créatifs comme le design, la musique ou les jeux.

**En une phrase** – L’IA peut renforcer la créativité humaine en proposant une large gamme d’idées qui inspirent une réflexion plus profonde et de meilleurs designs.
articles.personality_title_es: La IA ayuda a las personas a ser más creativas ofreciendo ideas diversas
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Muchas personas creen que la inteligencia artificial (IA) sirve principalmente para hacer tareas más rápido o reemplazar trabajos humanos. Pero una nueva investigación de la Universidad de Swansea muestra que la IA también puede ayudar a las personas a ser más creativas trabajando junto a ellas.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de Swansea pidieron a más de 800 personas diseñar autos virtuales usando un sistema especial de IA. Este sistema mostraba a los usuarios muchas ideas diferentes, incluyendo algunas inusuales o imperfectas. El estudio encontró que cuando las personas veían estas galerías creadas por la IA, pasaban más tiempo diseñando, se sentían más involucradas y hacían mejores diseños.

**Impacto** – Este estudio es importante porque muestra que la IA puede hacer más que solo acelerar el trabajo. Al ofrecer una variedad de ideas, incluso algunas “malas”, la IA ayuda a las personas a pensar de nuevas formas y a tomar riesgos creativos. También señala que las formas comunes de medir la ayuda de la IA, como contar clics, no muestran cómo la IA afecta el pensamiento y la creatividad.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores sugieren que las futuras herramientas de IA deben enfocarse en ofrecer ideas diversas para fomentar la creatividad. También quieren crear nuevas formas de medir cómo la IA influye en el pensamiento y la creación. Esto podría cambiar cómo se usa la IA en muchos trabajos creativos como diseño, música o juegos.

**Una frase clave** – La IA puede aumentar la creatividad humana ofreciendo una amplia variedad de ideas que inspiran un pensamiento más profundo y mejores diseños.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a glowing, intricate gallery wall filled with diverse, colorful car design sketches—ranging from sleek to quirky and imperfect—illuminated by soft light, with abstract silhouettes of human hands gently interacting and weaving threads of light between the designs, symbolizing creative collaboration and inspired exploration between humans and AI.

articles.title: How bioRxiv changed the way biologists share ideas – in numbers
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00709-3Four million articles are now downloaded from bioRxiv every month, according to an analysis of the life-science preprint server’s first 13 years of existence.
articles.content:
Neuroscience is the most represented field on the life-sciences preprint server bioRxiv.Credit: TEK Image/SPL

A comprehensive analysis of submissions to the preprint server bioRxiv has revealed a steady growth in the number of scientists uploading their findings to the platform — and neuroscientists have cemented their place as its top users.

In total, researchers have now posted more than 310,000 preprints to bioRxiv since it first launched in 2013, and the site receives about ten million views every month (see ‘The growth of bioRxiv’). The work also hints that the benefits of quick dissemination of research are winning over fears that the lack of peer review in preprints could cause a loss of rigorous quality control in scientific publishing.

“Posting and referencing bioRxiv papers has become so common these days that, in some fields, people can raise an eyebrow if you don’t post your work there, jokingly asking ‘Have they got something to hide?’,” says Richard Sever, chief science and strategy officer at openRxiv, who is based in New York City. Set up last year, openRxiv is the non-profit organization that operates bioRxiv and its sister site, the health-sciences preprint server medRxiv, which was launched in 2019.

Source: Ref. 1

Part of the routine

The analysis was posted on bioRxiv on 26 February by Sever and his colleagues1. It is an update of a paper posted in 2019, when the site had just hit 64,000 posted manuscripts.

“A clear message is the strong growth and interest in bioRxiv, with 4,000 papers published per month in 2025, alongside millions of monthly views and downloads,” says Katie Corker, executive director of ASAPbio, a non-profit organization in San Francisco, California, that works to improve transparency and rigour in communicating life-sciences research.

Since the server’s launch, monthly submissions have grown from a handful to more than 4,000 by late last year. About 80% of these preprints are published in research journals within three years, reports the analysis.

The analysis also includes results from a 2023 survey of more than 7,000 bioRxiv and medRxiv users, which revealed that 30% of authors post their preprints weeks to months before submitting to a journal, whereas 55% post around the time of journal submission. When asked how posting articles on bioRxiv has helped their careers, the majority (78%) said it had increased awareness of their research (see ‘Career accelerant’).

The server has secured itself as part of many scientists’ routines when scanning new research, says Sever. “Some people say that, if you don’t read bioRxiv, you’ll be a year behind everybody else in your field.”

Source: Ref. 1

Feedback culture

The survey also reveals that authors want to share work early, value the feedback they get and very rarely experience any negative consequences of posting preprints (see ‘Why post research on bioRxiv?’).

“There are people who are like, ‘I’ve done it. I’m going to put it out there, get some feedback and then I’ll go to a journal,’” says Sever. “There are definitely people I know who’ve said they’d put a paper on bioRxiv and have got really interesting feedback. It’s made the paper better, so that when they go to a journal, they have an easier ride.”

Source: Ref. 1

Some researchers still see preprint servers as bypassing the conventional quality-control processes of peer review because it has the potential to distort the public understanding of science. There are also concerns that preprint servers are seeing more submissions produced using artificial-intelligence tools, including a tide of AI slop.

To address such concerns, the bioRxiv team has made efforts to include a form of open peer review on the site.
articles.reasoning: The article highlights the significant positive impact of bioRxiv, a life-science preprint server, on accelerating scientific communication and collaboration. It shows broad adoption by the scientific community, increased research visibility, and improved feedback culture, which benefits the general public by speeding up the dissemination of scientific knowledge. The analysis provides detailed data on growth and usage, demonstrating substantial real-world benefits in research transparency and efficiency.
articles.personality_title: bioRxiv transforms how biologists share research with fast, open access
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – bioRxiv is a website where biologists and life-science researchers can share their new scientific papers before they are officially published in journals. It started in 2013 to help scientists share ideas quickly and get feedback from others.

**What happened** – A recent study looked at 13 years of data from bioRxiv. It found that over 310,000 research papers have been posted there, with about 4,000 new papers added each month in 2025. Neuroscientists are the biggest users. The site gets around 10 million views every month. Most of these preprints (about 80%) go on to be published in journals within three years. Many scientists use bioRxiv to share their work early and get helpful feedback to improve their papers.

**Impact** – bioRxiv has become a regular part of how scientists share and learn about new research. It helps spread scientific ideas faster than traditional publishing. This quick sharing encourages more collaboration and lets researchers get advice before formal review. Most users say posting on bioRxiv helps their work get noticed. Although some worry about quality control without peer review, bioRxiv has started adding open peer review to keep standards high.

**What's next step** – bioRxiv and its sister site medRxiv will keep growing as more scientists join. They plan to improve ways to check research quality, especially with new challenges like papers created by artificial intelligence. The sites will likely become even more important for sharing life-science discoveries quickly and openly.

**One-sentence takeaway** – bioRxiv has changed scientific publishing by helping biologists share and improve their work faster and more openly than ever before.
articles.personality_title_fr: bioRxiv transforme le partage des recherches en biologie grâce à un accès rapide et ouvert
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – bioRxiv est un site où les biologistes et chercheurs en sciences de la vie peuvent partager leurs nouveaux articles scientifiques avant leur publication officielle. Il a été lancé en 2013 pour aider les scientifiques à diffuser rapidement leurs idées et recevoir des retours.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Une étude récente a analysé 13 ans de données de bioRxiv. Elle montre que plus de 310 000 articles ont été déposés, avec environ 4 000 nouveaux articles chaque mois en 2025. Les neuroscientifiques sont les plus nombreux à utiliser la plateforme. Le site reçoit environ 10 millions de visites par mois. Environ 80 % de ces prépublications sont ensuite publiées dans des revues en trois ans. De nombreux chercheurs partagent leurs travaux tôt pour obtenir des conseils utiles.

**Impact** – bioRxiv est devenu une étape habituelle pour partager et découvrir les nouvelles recherches. Il accélère la diffusion des idées scientifiques, encourage la collaboration et permet d'améliorer les articles avant la revue officielle. La plupart des utilisateurs disent que cela aide à mieux faire connaître leurs travaux. Malgré des inquiétudes sur le contrôle de qualité, bioRxiv a commencé à intégrer une revue ouverte pour garantir des normes élevées.

**Prochaine étape** – bioRxiv et son site frère medRxiv vont continuer à grandir avec de nouveaux utilisateurs. Ils prévoient d'améliorer les moyens de vérifier la qualité des recherches, notamment face aux défis posés par l'intelligence artificielle. Ces plateformes deviendront encore plus importantes pour partager rapidement et ouvertement les découvertes en sciences de la vie.

**Phrase clé** – bioRxiv a transformé la publication scientifique en aidant les biologistes à partager et améliorer leurs travaux plus rapidement et plus ouvertement que jamais.
articles.personality_title_es: bioRxiv revoluciona cómo los biólogos comparten investigaciones con acceso rápido y abierto
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – bioRxiv es un sitio web donde los biólogos y científicos de la vida pueden compartir sus nuevos artículos científicos antes de que se publiquen oficialmente. Comenzó en 2013 para ayudar a los investigadores a difundir ideas rápido y recibir comentarios.

**Qué pasó** – Un estudio reciente analizó 13 años de datos de bioRxiv. Encontró que se han publicado más de 310,000 artículos, con alrededor de 4,000 nuevos cada mes en 2025. Los neurocientíficos son los usuarios principales. El sitio recibe alrededor de 10 millones de visitas mensuales. Cerca del 80 % de estos preprints se publican en revistas científicas en tres años. Muchos científicos usan bioRxiv para compartir su trabajo temprano y mejorar sus artículos con retroalimentación.

**Impacto** – bioRxiv se ha vuelto parte normal del proceso para compartir y conocer nuevas investigaciones. Ayuda a difundir ideas científicas más rápido que la publicación tradicional. Esto fomenta la colaboración y permite mejorar los trabajos antes de la revisión formal. La mayoría dice que publicar en bioRxiv ayuda a que su trabajo sea más conocido. Aunque hay preocupaciones sobre el control de calidad sin revisión por pares, bioRxiv ha empezado a usar revisiones abiertas para mantener altos estándares.

**Próximo paso** – bioRxiv y su sitio hermano medRxiv seguirán creciendo con más científicos. Planean mejorar la verificación de calidad, especialmente con retos como artículos creados por inteligencia artificial. Estos sitios serán cada vez más importantes para compartir descubrimientos de ciencias de la vida rápido y abiertamente.

**Frase clave** – bioRxiv ha cambiado la publicación científica al ayudar a los biólogos a compartir y mejorar su trabajo más rápido y con mayor apertura que nunca.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of an open book transforming into a vibrant neural network made of glowing, interconnected synapses, surrounded by floating manuscript pages gently rising like birds, set against a soft, natural-colored background symbolizing the flow and sharing of scientific ideas.

articles.title: No such thing as a shark? Genomes shake up ocean predator’s family tree
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00594-wSharks might not be a natural biological group, with most species potentially closer kin to rays than to an oddball group of sharks.
articles.content:
This great white shark (Carcharodon carcharias) is still a shark. Credit: Dave Fleetham/Design Pics Editorial/UIG via Getty

In a 1981 magazine essay, the evolutionary biologist Stephen Jay Gould let readers in one of his field’s counter-intuitive truths. Aquatic animals, including lungfish and coelacanths, are more closely related to tetrapods — four-limbed vertebrates — than to salmon, sticklebacks and many other things people call ‘fish’ — or, as Gould quipped, “there is surely no such thing as a fish”.

Sharks could be in a similar situation. A genomic study of dozens of shark species and their close relatives suggests that the ocean’s top predators might also not be a natural biological group, contrary to what studies using more-limited genetic data have suggested.

The analysis, posted last month on the bioRxiv preprint server, finds that, when researchers look at some ‘ultra-conserved’ parts of the genome, a peculiar family of sharks called Hexanchiformes might be part of an evolutionary lineage that is distinct from the group that includes all other sharks, as well as skates and rays1.

The results, which haven’t been peer reviewed, suggest that most animals that people call sharks are more closely related to rays and skates than to hexanchiform shark species — just as Gould pointed out was the case for some species called fishes. Biologists call such groups paraphyletic.

Whether a grouping of animals is paraphyletic or not doesn’t keep most scientists awake at night. But accurate family trees, or phylogenies — including one for sharks — help researchers to chart the evolution of key traits. “Having an accurate phylogeny is a way forward for understanding the processes that have shaped life,” says Gavin Naylor, an evolutionary biologist at the Florida Museum of Natural History in Gainesville.

Shifting definitions

Sharks, along with rays, skates and other sea creatures with a cartilaginous skeleton, are part of a group called Chondrichthyes, which shared a common ancestor with bony fish that lived more than 400 million years ago.

Animals resembling modern sharks have existed for much of this time, says Naylor. “These things have been around looking the way they look, or at least recognizable as sharks, for 330 million years.”

Yet researchers are unsure how the different members of Chondrichthyes — which is one of the major jawed-vertebrate groups — are related to one another. Anatomical studies have concluded2 that rays and skates, together known as batoids, were either distinct from all sharks, or members of a shark subgroup.

What were the first animals? The fierce sponge–jelly battle that just won’t end

Studies of limited genetic data have also tended to class sharks and batoids as distinct evolutionary groups. Increasingly, researchers are redrawing the animal tree of life using entire genomes — sometimes with bewildering and controversial conclusions. But sharks have yet to receive such attention.

To plug this gap, evolutionary biologists Thomas Near and Chase Brownstein at Yale University in New Haven, Connecticut, analysed previously published genomes of 48 species of shark, ray and other animals representing all the major lineages of Chondrichthyes. They looked at two kinds of data: 840 protein-coding sequences that are shared across species and around 350 ‘ultra-conserved’ regions, which are slowly evolving sequences that probably serve important, but often unknown, functions.

The protein-coding genes confirmed existing ‘monophyletic’ shark family trees, with rays and skates classed as distinct from all sharks. The ultra-conserved sequences, however, suggested that sharks were paraphyletic: the hexanchiform sharks formed a lineage distinct from the one that gave rise to all other sharks, rays and skates. Hexanchiformes, which include cow sharks and frilled sharks, have six or seven gill slits instead of the usual five, as well as primitive-looking jaws and eel-like bodies.

One implication of this tree is that flat-bodied animals, including manta rays, evolved from shark-like ancestors. “This result implies that rays and skates are just another type of shark, and that the shark body plan came first,” says Brownstein.

He and Near favour the hypothesis that sharks are paraphyletic, in part because this tree was more strongly supported by their analysis than was a monophyletic one. Sequencing more shark species and looking at other types of genetic marker might be needed to determine which of the two trees is correct.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific discovery using advanced genomic analysis that challenges long-held biological classifications of sharks, reshaping understanding of evolutionary relationships. This breakthrough in genetic research has broad implications for evolutionary biology and biodiversity knowledge, benefiting the scientific community and public understanding of marine life.
articles.personality_title: New genetic study reshapes shark family tree
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Sharks have long been seen as one group of ocean predators, separate from rays and skates. But scientists have been unsure about exactly how these animals are related to each other.

**What happened** – Researchers at Yale University studied the complete genomes of 48 species of sharks, rays, and related animals. They found that a special family of sharks called Hexanchiformes is actually quite different from other sharks. This means that most sharks are more closely related to rays and skates than to these unusual sharks.

**Impact** – This discovery changes how scientists understand shark evolution. It suggests that rays and skates evolved from shark-like ancestors, making the traditional idea of sharks as one clear group incorrect. This new family tree helps explain how different features, like body shape, developed over millions of years.

**What's next step** – Scientists will study more shark species and use different genetic methods to confirm these findings. This ongoing research will improve knowledge of ocean life and how these animals evolved.

**One-sentence takeaway** – A new genome study shows that sharks are not one single group but have a surprising family connection to rays and skates, changing how we see their evolution.
articles.personality_title_fr: Une nouvelle étude génétique bouleverse l’arbre généalogique des requins
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les requins ont longtemps été considérés comme un groupe unique de prédateurs marins, distinct des raies et des poissons-papillons. Mais les scientifiques ne savaient pas exactement comment ces animaux étaient liés.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’université de Yale ont étudié les génomes complets de 48 espèces de requins, de raies et d’animaux apparentés. Ils ont découvert qu’une famille spéciale de requins appelée Hexanchiformes est en réalité très différente des autres requins. Cela signifie que la plupart des requins sont plus proches des raies et des poissons-papillons que de ces requins particuliers.

**Impact** – Cette découverte change la compréhension de l’évolution des requins. Elle suggère que les raies et les poissons-papillons ont évolué à partir d’ancêtres ressemblant à des requins, ce qui remet en question l’idée traditionnelle d’un groupe unique de requins. Ce nouvel arbre généalogique aide à expliquer comment différentes caractéristiques, comme la forme du corps, se sont développées sur des millions d’années.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques étudieront plus d’espèces de requins et utiliseront d’autres méthodes génétiques pour confirmer ces résultats. Ces recherches vont améliorer la connaissance de la vie marine et de l’évolution de ces animaux.

**En résumé** – Une nouvelle étude génomique montre que les requins ne forment pas un groupe unique, mais sont étroitement liés aux raies et poissons-papillons, ce qui change notre vision de leur évolution.
articles.personality_title_es: Nuevo estudio genético cambia el árbol familiar de los tiburones
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Durante mucho tiempo, los tiburones se han visto como un grupo único de depredadores marinos, separados de las rayas y las mantarrayas. Pero los científicos no estaban seguros de cómo se relacionaban exactamente estos animales.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad de Yale estudiaron los genomas completos de 48 especies de tiburones, rayas y animales relacionados. Encontraron que una familia especial llamada Hexanchiformes es bastante diferente de otros tiburones. Esto significa que la mayoría de los tiburones están más relacionados con las rayas y mantarrayas que con estos tiburones inusuales.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento cambia cómo los científicos entienden la evolución de los tiburones. Sugiere que las rayas y mantarrayas evolucionaron de ancestros similares a tiburones, haciendo que la idea tradicional de los tiburones como un grupo único sea incorrecta. Este nuevo árbol familiar ayuda a explicar cómo se desarrollaron diferentes características, como la forma del cuerpo, durante millones de años.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos estudiarán más especies de tiburones y usarán otros métodos genéticos para confirmar estos hallazgos. Esta investigación continua mejorará el conocimiento sobre la vida marina y su evolución.

**Resumen en una frase** – Un nuevo estudio genómico muestra que los tiburones no forman un solo grupo, sino que tienen una conexión familiar sorprendente con las rayas y mantarrayas, cambiando nuestra visión de su evolución.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a great white shark and a frilled shark gracefully swimming side by side in a calm ocean, their contrasting body shapes and multiple gill slits highlighted, with subtle, flowing DNA strands weaving between them to symbolize their complex evolutionary relationship.
